evidence: Guard clauses are correctly placed before processing (e.g., null/empty lemma guards in executeCollocations and executeSurfacePattern return early before any computation)
Multi-phase algorithms (exploration, BCQL query) are decomposed into helper methods with single responsibilities, making control flow easy to follow and verify

In ExploreResponseAssembler.buildExplorationEdges, the outer loop iterates result.seedCollocates() which in multi-seed mode is the UNION of ALL seeds' collocates (merged via Math::max in MultiSeedExplorer.findCollocateIntersection), then the inner loop creates one SEED_ADJ edge per seed for every collocate — producing a full cross-product. Collocates belonging to only one seed generate spurious edges to all other seeds with incorrect max-logDice weights.

ExplorationHandlers.parseSeedSet is annotated @NonNull on its parameter, yet the method immediately checks `if (seedsParam == null || seedsParam.isEmpty())` — the null branch is unreachable under the declared contract, sending mixed signals to callers and readers about whether null is expected.

strengths: Guard clauses are consistently placed before computation: null/empty lemma checks in executeCollocations, executeSurfacePattern, and executeDependencyPattern all return empty lists before touching the index
Boolean conditions are direct and non-redundant throughout — no identical if/else branches, no always-true conditions, no `if x return true else return false` patterns were found
The two-phase BCQL hit-collection pipeline (collectHits → scoreHits) separates concerns cleanly and each conditional inside (null concordance, null parts, empty collocate) has distinct semantics
Exception routing is logically consistent: IllegalArgumentException for client validation errors (caught as 400), IOException for index failures (caught as 500), with no overlapping catch blocks
logic_clarity.issue_character: Issues are isolated rather than systemic: one semantic logic error in multi-seed edge construction (cross-product vs per-seed actual edges) and one minor annotation-versus-implementation inconsistency; they do not reflect a broad pattern of unclear or incorrect logic.
logic_clarity.score_rationale: The codebase handles control flow correctly and clearly across nearly all paths — guard clauses are well-placed, conditions are not redundant, and multi-step algorithms decompose into verifiable phases. The main substantive defect is in ExploreResponseAssembler.buildExplorationEdges where multi-seed mode produces SEED_ADJ edges connecting every seed to every collocate in the union set, regardless of actual per-seed association; this makes the multi-seed graph semantically incorrect (misrepresented edges with max-of-all-seeds logDice weights). The defect is confined to one method and one code path, but it silently produces wrong output for a featured use-case. This pulls the score below the 'solid but uneven' 80 mark while the otherwise clean logic holds it well above the 60 'significant drag' threshold.

issues.summary: buildExplorationEdges creates SEED_ADJ cross-product in multi-seed mode, connecting every seed to every collocate regardless of actual per-seed association
evidence: MultiSeedExplorer.findCollocateIntersection builds seedCollocScores as a UNION of all seeds' collocates via `seedCollocScores.merge(wsr.lemma(), wsr.logDice(), Math::max)` — this map contains every collocate from any seed

ExploreResponseAssembler.buildExplorationEdges outer loop: `for (Map.Entry<String, Double> colloc : result.seedCollocates().entrySet())` — iterates all union collocates; inner loop: `for (String seed : seedList)` — creates one SEED_ADJ edge per (collocate, seed) pair
For seeds [theory, model, hypothesis]: if 'empirical' collocates only with 'theory', the loop still emits edges model→empirical and hypothesis→empirical with theory's max logDice weight

DISCOVERED_ADJ edges (from discoveredNouns) correctly scope to per-seed actual collocates via buildDiscoveredNouns, creating an inconsistency between SEED_ADJ and DISCOVERED_ADJ semantics in the same response
issues.suggestion: In buildExplorationEdges, replace the cross-product loop with per-seed collocate data. For multi-seed mode ExplorationResult, the per-seed actual collocates are available in discoveredNouns (each seed is a DiscoveredNoun in multi-seed mode); emit SEED_ADJ edges from `noun.sharedCollocates()` for each discovered noun that is a seed. Alternatively, store a per-seed collocate map in ExplorationResult so buildExplorationEdges can iterate seed→its_actual_collocates without relying on the union map.

issues.summary: parseSeedSet annotates its parameter @NonNull but immediately checks for null, creating an unreachable branch that contradicts the declared contract
evidence: Method signature: `private static Set<String> parseSeedSet(@NonNull String seedsParam)`
First line of body: `if (seedsParam == null || seedsParam.isEmpty()) return Set.of();` — the null branch cannot be reached under the @NonNull contract
All three call sites pass the result of HttpApiUtils.requireParam which already throws IllegalArgumentException on blank input, making the isEmpty() branch also unreachable at those call sites
issues.suggestion: Remove the null check since the @NonNull annotation and all callers guarantee a non-null value; either keep the isEmpty() guard (with a comment if the method is meant to tolerate empty strings) or document that callers are responsible for non-empty input. If null tolerance is actually required, remove the @NonNull annotation.

root_causes: The multi-seed SEED_ADJ cross-product likely originated when the single-seed edge-building loop (one seed × its collocates) was extended to multi-seed by adding an inner loop over seeds, without accounting for the fact that seedCollocates is now a union rather than a per-seed map
likely_symptoms: multi_seed_seed_adj_cross_product — the graph over-reports connections in multi-seed visualizations
